label: Seed Viability
description: Evaluate seed lot viability, germination strength, and replacement timing so garden, irrigation, and yield plans stay realistic.

outputs.description: Per-lot viability scores, adjusted sowing rates, and replacement flags used across the Planning Graph.

sections.label: Seed Lots & Details
sections.description: Enter crop, variety, year, and storage details for each seed lot.

sections.label: Storage Conditions
sections.description: Capture how and where seeds are stored (cool, dark, dry, etc.).

sections.label: Germination Tests
sections.description: Optional quick tests to refine viability estimates.

sections.label: Results & Recommendations
sections.description: Review viability scores, sowing multipliers, and replacement timing.

sessionTemplates.label: Quick Germination Test
sessionTemplates.description: Run a paper towel or tray-based germination test on selected lots.
stepHints: Gather labeled samples from each seed lot.
Prepare moist paper towel or starter tray.
Record germinated seeds over several days in SSA.

sessionTemplates.label: Sort & Cull Seed Lots
sessionTemplates.description: Use viability results to keep, separate, or cull lots before planting season.
stepHints: Separate seed lots by viability status.
Mark low-viability lots for heavy sowing or culling.
Update SSA with which lots you will keep or replace.

mappings.description: Next Steps mapping to Garden Yield, Storehouse, and Irrigation planning flows.

guards.notes: Sessions spawned from this node should respect Sabbath and quiet hour constraints when scheduling hands-on work.

notes: If no storage data is available, default assumptions are conservative.
Very old lots may still be used at higher sow rates, but marked as high risk.
Replace critical crops (staples) sooner than minor experimentals when viability is low.

nextBestActions: Plan germination test sessions for low-confidence lots.
Increase sow rate for medium viability lots in key beds.
Schedule seed replacement purchases before critical planting windows.
planningGraph.notes: Seed viability quality is a foundational input for realistic yield expectations, irrigation planning, and storehouse stocking goals.
